vendors.name: AutoCount
vendors.tagline: All-in-one accounting, POS, payroll & e-Invoice for Malaysian SMEs
vendors.market_position: One of Malaysia's two dominant SME accounting platforms (alongside SQL). KL-based and Malaysia-native, with 240,000+ businesses and a deep reseller network, in on-premise and cloud editions.
vendors.typical_pricing: ~RM1,599–RM2,899 one-off (desktop); extra network user ~RM800; Cloud tiers separate.
vendors.my_specific_note: Direct MyInvois/LHDN submission is built in (standard, consolidated and self-billed) via its "AIP" platform with a retry mechanism for LHDN downtime — and e-Invoice is included at no extra cost, with native SST handling.

vendors.name: SQL Account
vendors.tagline: Leading accounting & payroll software in Malaysia, LHDN e-Invoice ready
vendors.market_position: The other half of Malaysia's SME accounting duopoly — very widely deployed and used as a training standard, in on-prem and SQL Cloud editions.
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based via resellers (desktop one-off + SQL Cloud subscription tiers).
vendors.my_specific_note: One-click MyInvois submission is built in (XML/JSON to LHDN with a QR validation), once your ERP is registered with MyInvois — with full SST support and Malaysian localization.

vendors.name: Financio (by ABSS)
vendors.tagline: Cloud accounting built for Malaysian SMEs and non-accountants
vendors.market_position: The cloud product from ABSS (formerly MYOB South Asia), a long-established Malaysian accounting vendor — aimed at startups and small businesses that want simplicity.
pros: Backed by the established ABSS/MYOB lineage
cons: Lighter feature set than SQL/AutoCount for complex needs
vendors.typical_pricing: Subscription tiers (Free / Essentials / Premier).
vendors.my_specific_note: Handles MyInvois e-invoicing via API on the Essentials and Premier tiers (generating XML/JSON and submitting to LHDN), and pairs with ABSS desktop for existing users — SST-ready throughout.

vendors.name: Bukku
vendors.tagline: Cloud accounting with a permanently free LHDN e-Invoice plan
vendors.market_position: A modern Malaysian cloud-accounting challenger, distributed via UOB Business, that positions on price and free e-invoicing.
pros: Supports both MyInvois and the Peppol network
Eligible for the SME digitalisation grant
cons: Thinner for heavy inventory or construction job-costing
vendors.typical_pricing: Free invoicing tier; accounting from <RM2/day (~RM600–700/yr); grant can subsidise 50%.
vendors.my_specific_note: A strong MyInvois angle — it integrates both LHDN MyInvois and the Peppol network and markets a permanently free e-invoicing plan, and qualifying SMEs can claim the government 50% / up-to-RM5,000 digitalisation grant.

vendors.name: Biztory Cloud Accounting
vendors.tagline: Cloud accounting & invoicing on the go for Malaysian small business
vendors.market_position: A Malaysian cloud-accounting SaaS (since 2014) with a budget-friendly, SME/micro focus.
vendors.typical_pricing: From ~RM390/year (entry tier).
vendors.my_specific_note: Automatic LHDN-compliant MyInvois submission (it consolidates and submits to avoid manual portal entry), with SST handling built in — a low-cost way for a micro-business to get compliant.

vendors.name: Sage UBS (Sage Malaysia)
vendors.tagline: Long-running localized accounting (UBS) plus mid-market Sage 300/X3
vendors.market_position: Sage UBS has been a Malaysian SME staple since 1988 (now also branded Access UBS), while Sage 300/X3 serve larger multi-entity firms needing job costing.
vendors.reputation_flag: Sage's direction for the "UBS" line has shifted under the Access Group acquisition — confirm the current product/branding and support continuity before relying on the legacy UBS product.
pros: Deep legacy install base + partner network
cons: Branding/ownership confusion (Sage UBS vs Access UBS)
E-invoicing may need a third-party Access Point (e.g. SESAMi)
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based (UBS one-off via partners; 300/X3 enterprise quote).
vendors.my_specific_note: MyInvois/LHDN compliance is available, with Sage 300 routing e-invoices to LHDN via SESAMi as an Access Point — and the job-costing modules suit construction and multi-department contractors.

vendors.name: HashMicro
vendors.tagline: Cloud ERP for Asia-Pacific SMEs, MyInvois-ready, with a construction module
vendors.market_position: A Southeast-Asia ERP vendor (Singapore-based, with a strong Malaysia marketing presence) serving retail, manufacturing, construction and services with industry-specific configs and AI automation.
vendors.reputation_flag: Self-ranking conflict — HashMicro places ITSELF at #1 in its own "best ERP / best e-invoicing software" listicles, which rank prominently in Malaysian search; treat its comparison content as marketing, not independent review.
pros: Broad ERP breadth incl. a construction module
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based (per-module / per-user enterprise quote).
vendors.my_specific_note: Markets as fully MyInvois-ready with a direct LHDN connection and SST compliance, and offers a construction/project module — just read its own "best of" rankings (where it crowns itself) as marketing, not a neutral review.

vendors.name: BuildSpace (BuildSpace Pro)
vendors.tagline: "A construction digital solution made by QS for QS"
vendors.market_position: A Malaysia-native (since 2016) end-to-end cloud platform for developers, contractors and consultants — BQ, e-tendering, contract management and progress claims. A standout local quantity-surveying tool.
cons: Not itself an e-invoice submitter — pairs with accounting software
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based (module/seat-based).
vendors.my_specific_note: Directly relevant to the construction e-invoicing reality — it handles the progress-claim certificates and variation orders linked to contract BQ items, exactly the documents construction firms must e-invoice individually under MyInvois. Pair it with an accounting tool for the LHDN submission itself.

vendors.name: Cubicost by Glodon
vendors.tagline: BIM-based 5D quantity takeoff and cost estimating for construction
vendors.market_position: Glodon's Cubicost suite (TAS/TRB/TBQ/TME) for BIM quantity takeoff and 5D cost management, with a Malaysia entity plus local resellers and documented Malaysian project case studies.
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based via Malaysian resellers (per-module: TAS/TRB/TBQ).
vendors.my_specific_note: Used by Malaysian QS and contractors for BQ and estimating, with Glodon Malaysia providing local sales and support — but it is a takeoff/estimating tool, not an LHDN/MyInvois one, so it pairs with accounting software.

vendors.name: RIB CostX
vendors.tagline: 2D & BIM takeoff and estimating with live-linked workbooks
vendors.market_position: A global estimating/QS standard (originally Exactal, now RIB Software), available in Malaysia via reseller and used by QS firms operating in and across the country.
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based (premium; per-license).
vendors.my_specific_note: Sold into Malaysia through local resellers for BQ, estimating and tender prep — a strong international QS brand, but not Malaysia-native and not a MyInvois tool, so it's purely the takeoff/estimating layer.

vendors.name: Globe3 ERP
vendors.tagline: Cloud ERP with a dedicated construction/project-accounting module
vendors.market_position: An ERP suite (Singapore-HQ, with a Malaysia office and a localized MY site) with 20+ years' track record, serving construction, trading, manufacturing and field service — a solid mid-market construction ERP option locally.
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based.
vendors.my_specific_note: Markets a MyInvois API integration (handling the direct LHDN connection for clients) and project-accounting for tracking subcontractors, materials and expenses — a fit for the construction no-consolidation reality, with a local MY office for support.

vendors.name: IFCA MSC Berhad
vendors.tagline: Property & construction enterprise software, Bursa Malaysia-listed
vendors.market_position: An established Malaysian software house (founded 1987, listed on Bursa Malaysia since 2003) with 800+ clients, specializing in property developers and contractors — its ContractX handles construction and contract management.
cons: Aimed more at developers than micro-SMEs
Legacy elements in the product line
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-based (enterprise).
vendors.my_specific_note: Malaysia-listed and deeply localized for property/construction — its ContractX targets the progress claims and contract management relevant to the MyInvois construction rules, with local SST/LHDN compliance.

vendors.name: ePerolehan (Government eProcurement)
vendors.tagline: Malaysia's official federal e-procurement portal for government tenders
vendors.market_position: The mandatory federal eProcurement system — all federal supply/service procurement runs through it, and ministries and agencies advertise tenders, quotations and contracts here. The single largest public source of government work for contractors.
pros: Authoritative, mandatory source of federal contracts
cons: Not a curated "alert" tool on its own
vendors.typical_pricing: Free Basic Account (transacts up to RM20,000); MOF Account RM450 / 3 years for more.
vendors.my_specific_note: Suppliers register by category (including Contractor), and CIDB-graded contractors bid on works here — though major construction-works tenders are also routed via CIDB's own e-tender portal.

vendors.name: CIDB e-Tender & N3C
vendors.tagline: CIDB's national construction tender portal + cost-data centre
vendors.market_position: CIDB (the construction-industry regulator) runs a dedicated e-tender portal where major works tenders from bodies like JKR/PWD are advertised to CIDB-registered contractors, plus N3C (the National Construction Cost Centre) with a Tender Price Index.
cons: Not a polished SaaS alert product
vendors.typical_pricing: Free (government portal); N3C data products vary.
vendors.my_specific_note: A construction-specific lead source aligned to CIDB grading — it complements ePerolehan for works tenders, and the N3C provides Malaysian construction cost benchmarks (the Tender Price Index) useful for bidding.

vendors.name: Tenderpanel
vendors.tagline: Malaysia's government procurement intelligence platform
vendors.market_position: The strongest genuine private Malaysia-native tender-intelligence SaaS — it consolidates public (CIDB, MOF, UPKJ) and private tenders plus quotations into one searchable database with daily keyword alerts.
pros: Consolidates many sources into one feed
cons: Data completeness vs official portals unverified
vendors.typical_pricing: Basic + Pro subscription tiers (RM pricing not published).
vendors.my_specific_note: Built around Malaysian licence categories (CIDB, MOF/KKM, PUKONSA, UPKJ filtering), and its Pro tier adds award intelligence — the winning contractor, exact RM award value and contract period for past government contracts — a real competitive-bidding edge.

vendors.name: Recommend.my
vendors.tagline: Malaysia's home-improvement & contractor marketplace
vendors.market_position: A leading Malaysian home-services/renovation marketplace (since 2015), active in 2026 with 10,000+ vetted pros — renovation contractors, interior designers, handymen and repair trades. A real lead channel for renovation/trades businesses.
pros: Verified reviews + money-back guarantee build trust
Genuine inbound lead flow for trades
cons: Provider economics (commission/lead fees) not publicly itemized
vendors.typical_pricing: Free for homeowners; provider lead/commission model (not publicly itemized).
vendors.my_specific_note: The strongest surviving consumer-to-contractor lead marketplace in Malaysia (after Kaodim closed in 2022) — local pros bid on homeowner projects, with verified reviews and past-project photos, on a lead/commission model.

vendors.name: Allianz General Malaysia
vendors.tagline: Global insurer, Malaysia's engineering heavyweight
vendors.market_position: One of the largest general insurers in Malaysia and part of the global Allianz Group, giving deep reinsurance capacity for large infrastructure. Engineering (CAR, EAR, machinery) is a flagship line, strongly broker/corporate-distributed.
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-only; CAR market benchmark ~0.08–0.30% of contract value (indicative, not an Allianz tariff).
vendors.my_specific_note: Its CAR explicitly allows add-ons for Construction Plant & Equipment, Machinery, Professional Fees and the Principal's Existing Property — well-suited to mid/large Malaysian projects, and placed through brokers rather than online.

vendors.name: Lonpac Insurance
vendors.tagline: Malaysia's home-grown engineering-class specialist
vendors.market_position: The operating arm of LPI Capital and a leading domestic insurer consistently cited among the top CAR/engineering carriers in Malaysia — it publishes its own CAR wordings and Product Disclosure Sheets.
pros: Dedicated Engineering & Machinery line incl. CAR
cons: Smaller global balance sheet than Allianz/Zurich for mega-projects
Sparse product pages (need the PDF PDS)
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-only; rated on sum insured/contract value; market band ~0.08–0.30% (indicative).
vendors.my_specific_note: A Malaysian-owned default market for contractors' CAR/EAR — strongly broker- and agency-distributed, reaching SME and bumiputera-contractor business through its nationwide agency force.

vendors.name: MSIG Malaysia
vendors.tagline: Japanese-backed (MS&AD) commercial engineering insurer
vendors.market_position: Part of the Mitsui Sumitomo / MS&AD global group, carrying a full commercial/engineering suite including Contractors' All Risks — a recognised CAR market in Malaysia with PIDM-protected products.
pros: Established CAR/engineering with a published PDS
cons: Engineering buried under "Other Commercial Insurance" (low discoverability)
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-only; % of contract value/sum insured; ~0.08–0.30% (indicative).
vendors.my_specific_note: Broker- and corporate-distributed and often favoured for projects with Japanese or multinational principals — it publishes a CAR Product Disclosure Sheet, though engineering sits under a generic "Other Commercial" menu.

vendors.name: Zurich General Malaysia
vendors.tagline: Global Zurich brand — with a Shariah CAR/EAR Takaful option
vendors.market_position: The local arm of the global Zurich Group, offering a combined CAR/EAR all-risks product covering both civil and M&E works — and running a parallel Takaful range (CAR Takaful, EAR Takaful).
cons: Two parallel ranges (conventional vs Takaful) can confuse buyers
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-only; rated on contract value/sum insured; ~0.08–0.30% (indicative).
vendors.my_specific_note: The conventional-plus-Takaful pairing is a genuine Malaysia differentiator for bumiputera, Islamic-finance-linked and government-linked projects that need a Shariah-compliant CAR/EAR.

vendors.name: Tokio Marine Malaysia
vendors.tagline: Engineering CAR/EAR and professional indemnity under one insurer
vendors.market_position: The local arm of the global Tokio Marine group — one of the few here with both a documented combined CAR/EAR product and a clearly stated PI line underwritten by the Malaysian entity.
pros: Good for bundling works + professional liability
cons: Smaller Malaysian general-insurance footprint than Allianz/Lonpac
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-only; CAR/EAR on contract value, PI on fee income + limit of indemnity; ~0.08–0.30% (indicative).
vendors.my_specific_note: A single-carrier option for a contractor who also needs Professional Indemnity for their design consultants on a Malaysian project — both CAR/EAR and PI are underwritten by the local entity, via brokers.

vendors.name: SQL Payroll
vendors.tagline: LHDN-verified Malaysian payroll with auto EPF, SOCSO, EIS, PCB and HRDF
vendors.market_position: The dominant Malaysia-only payroll incumbent, paired with the widely-deployed SQL Account. Strong with accountants and traditional SMEs wanting a locally-certified system, and heavily reseller-distributed.
pros: Uses LHDN's own PCB calculator engine
cons: Best value bundled with SQL Account
vendors.typical_pricing: Not published; commonly from ~RM799 one-time via reseller.
vendors.my_specific_note: Uses the official LHDN PCB calculator for 100%-matched MTD and generates the full statutory pack (EPF Borang A, SOCSO Borang 2/3/8A, EIS, Form EA, Borang E/CP8D, CP39/CP22) with text-file exports for KWSP/PERKESO/LHDN and bank uploads.

vendors.name: AutoCount Cloud Payroll
vendors.tagline: Cloud HR & payroll for Malaysian SMEs — LHDN/PCB/EPF/SOCSO/EIS/HRDF compliant
vendors.market_position: The payroll arm of AutoCount, the other major Malaysian SME accounting vendor — a cloud-native HR+payroll bundling e-Leave/e-Claim/e-Attendance, sold through a large dealer ecosystem.
cons: Payroll depth lighter than a dedicated HRIS
Best value inside the AutoCount ecosystem
Advanced HR gated to paid tiers
vendors.typical_pricing: Free up to 3 employees; paid from ~RM44/month.
vendors.my_specific_note: Fully compliant with LHDN/PCB/EPF/SOCSO/EIS/HRDF and auto-updates statutory rates (including Zakat) through to submission — a natural pick if you already run AutoCount Accounting.

vendors.name: Talenox
vendors.tagline: Clean cloud payroll & leave across Malaysia, Singapore and Hong Kong
vendors.market_position: A regional SaaS popular with startups and lean SMEs for its clean UI and fast onboarding, on a self-serve online model rather than reseller-led.
vendors.typical_pricing: Base RM40 for first 5 headcount + RM8/full-timer; promo annual ~RM5/employee/mo.
vendors.my_specific_note: Auto-calculates EPF/SOCSO/EIS on each payroll run and produces PCB/MTD plus statutory exports — a good fit for a modern contractor with staff across Malaysia, Singapore or Hong Kong.

vendors.name: Kakitangan.com
vendors.tagline: Malaysia-first HR & payroll built around local statutory workflows
vendors.market_position: One of the earliest, most recognised Malaysia-focused cloud HR/payroll brands, with a micro/small-business focus built on local fit and affordability.
cons: Lighter feature set than enterprise HRIS
Micro/SME orientation may not suit larger firms
vendors.typical_pricing: From ~RM50/month for up to 5 employees.
vendors.my_specific_note: Handles full statutory submissions (EPF/SOCSO/EIS/PCB/HRDF) plus the EA Form and LHDN e-filing — a cheap, local-first option for a small contractor making its first payroll hire.

vendors.name: BrioHR
vendors.tagline: All-in-one Malaysian HRMS & payroll, recruitment to payslip
vendors.market_position: A KL-HQ'd (founded 2018) Malaysia-born modular HRMS that raised a ~US$6.5M Series A and is expanding across ASEAN — aimed at growing SMEs that want a full HR suite, not just payroll.
pros: Well-funded local company with regional ambition
cons: Broad suite is more than a payroll-only buyer needs
vendors.typical_pricing: Custom/quotation-based.
vendors.my_specific_note: Fully LHDN-compliant — it streamlines EPF/SOCSO/EIS/HRDF/PCB and Malaysian public holidays inside a broader recruitment-to-payslip platform, fitting a contractor scaling its back office.

vendors.name: CIDB Malaysia
vendors.tagline: Mandatory contractor registration & construction-personnel certification (Act 520)
vendors.market_position: The federal statutory body under Act 520 (CIDB Act 1994) that regulates Malaysia's construction industry — contractor registration and G1–G7 grading (SPKK), the Green Card, accreditation and standards, via the CIMS portal.
pros: Legally enables you to tender for and undertake construction work
G1–G7 grade sets tender capacity (G1 ≤RM200k … G7 unlimited)
Green Card certifies site personnel as safety-inducted
cons: Registration compulsory BEFORE any work — operating unregistered risks RM10,000–RM100,000 fine (s.29)
Cannot tender/work after the certificate expires until renewed
Cannot exceed your grade ceiling or work outside your registered category
Every worker needs a Green Card (penalties up to ~RM5,000 per worker without)
vendors.typical_pricing: Green Card RM35 (1yr) → RM135 (5yr) via CIMS; contractor SPKK fees vary by grade/category.
vendors.my_specific_note: Under s.25(1) no one may undertake construction work as a contractor without valid CIDB registration, and under s.33(1) every worker, supervisor and manager on site must hold a valid Green Card (Kad Hijau) — the two legal preconditions to bid or build. Grades also require minimum paid-up capital (G1 ~RM5k → G7 >RM750k).

vendors.name: Board of Engineers Malaysia (BEM)
vendors.tagline: Statutory registration of engineers & engineering practices (REA 1967)
vendors.market_position: The statutory body under the Registration of Engineers Act 1967 (Rev. 2015), formed in 1972, that registers engineers, engineering technologists, inspectors of works and consultancy practices, and regulates professional conduct.
pros: Legal authority to practise and submit engineering work
Professional Engineer (Ir.) status to endorse designs and sign off submissions
Lets you register an engineering consultancy practice
Professional standing for tenders that require registered engineers
cons: PE status needs ≥3 years post-grad experience + the Professional Assessment Exam
vendors.typical_pricing: Varies by category — see the official BEM site.
vendors.my_specific_note: For construction/infrastructure requiring engineering input the engineer must be BEM-registered, and only a registered Professional Engineer (Ir.) may submit or endorse engineering work — a Graduate Engineer needs ≥3 years supervised experience first.

vendors.name: SSM (Companies Commission of Malaysia)
vendors.tagline: Statutory registration of companies & businesses in Malaysia
vendors.market_position: The regulatory authority for companies (Companies Act 2016), businesses (ROB Act 1956) and LLPs — registration is mandatory to operate legally, via ezBiz (sole prop/partnership) and MyCoID (Sdn Bhd).
pros: Establishes a legal entity (Sdn Bhd, sole prop, partnership, LLP)
Prerequisite for business bank accounts, CIDB registration and tendering
Fast for sole props (~1–3 working days)
cons: Annual renewal for enterprises/sole props (lapses if missed)
Sdn Bhd carries ongoing obligations (annual return, audited accounts, company secretary)
Paid-up capital matters for CIDB grade minimums
Sdn Bhd incorporation can take up to ~2 weeks
vendors.typical_pricing: Sole prop RM30/yr (personal name) or RM60/yr (trade name); Sdn Bhd RM1,000 one-time; LLP RM500.
vendors.my_specific_note: A contractor must be an SSM-registered legal entity (Sdn Bhd or enterprise/sole prop) before registering with CIDB or bidding — it is the foundational legal-entity step, and your paid-up capital here feeds your CIDB grade ceiling.

vendors.name: LHDN / IRBM (Inland Revenue Board)
vendors.tagline: Income-tax registration, the MyTax portal and MyInvois e-invoicing
vendors.market_position: The federal authority administering direct taxes (Income Tax Act 1967) under the Ministry of Finance — it runs the MyTax gateway and the MyInvois e-invoicing platform.
pros: Issues the TIN required for all legal/financial dealings
MyInvois enables compliant e-invoicing for B2B/B2G construction billing
Employers handle PCB/MTD through LHDN channels
cons: MyInvois is phased by turnover band
Non-compliant invoices are penalised RM200–RM20,000 each (s.120(1)(d), ITA 1967)
Ongoing monthly PCB/MTD plus annual filing
TIN required before most commercial onboarding
vendors.typical_pricing: Free (TIN registration and MyInvois portal usage).
vendors.my_specific_note: A contractor must register for income tax and obtain a TIN via e-Daftar, then issue validated e-invoices through MyInvois — the RM1m–RM5m turnover band is in MyInvois scope from 1 Jan 2026, with full penalty enforcement extended to 1 Jan 2028. See our MyInvois guide for the construction specifics.

vendors.name: EPF / KWSP
vendors.tagline: Mandatory employer registration & retirement-savings contributions
vendors.market_position: The statutory retirement-savings body under the EPF Act 1991 — any employer hiring at least one employee must register and contribute, transacting via i-Akaun (Employer).
pros: Establishes the legal employer account to hire compliantly
i-Akaun for online employee registration, contributions and bank uploads
Baseline requirement for any contractor with workers
cons: Strict 7-day registration after the first hire
Monthly remittance (employer + employee) with late penalties
vendors.typical_pricing: No registration fee; statutory contribution rates apply.
vendors.my_specific_note: Under s.41(1) EPF Act 1991 an employer must register within 7 days of its first hire, activate i-Akaun within 30 days, then remit monthly — one of the tightest statutory deadlines a new contractor faces.

vendors.name: SOCSO / PERKESO
vendors.tagline: Mandatory employer registration for injury, invalidity & EIS protection
vendors.market_position: The statutory body under the Employees' Social Security Act 1969 and the EIS Act 2017 — employers register and contribute via the ASSIST portal. Especially load-bearing in construction given on-site injury risk.
pros: Employment-injury, invalidity and EIS coverage (critical for construction risk)
A single ASSIST portal for combined SOCSO + EIS
Covers foreign workers (Employment Injury since 2019, Invalidity since 2024)
cons: Non-compliance risks fines up to RM10,000 and/or 2 years' imprisonment
Statutory scope/rates — you cannot opt out
vendors.typical_pricing: No registration fee; statutory rates apply.
vendors.my_specific_note: An employer must register within 30 days of hiring (Form 1 / ASSIST) and contribute monthly for Employment Injury & Invalidity plus EIS by the 15th — and because the mandatory CIDB Green Card bundles only a basic personal-accident Takaful, SOCSO is the real injury backbone on site.

vendors.name: HRD Corp
vendors.tagline: Training-levy registration for employers with 10+ employees
vendors.market_position: The statutory body under the PSMB Act 2001 that manages the HR-development levy and training-grant ecosystem, with registration via the eTRiS portal.
pros: The levy is reclaimable as training grants (e.g. CIDB/safety/technical courses)
Supports workforce upskilling for a growing contractor
Funds the safety training the industry needs
cons: Failure to register risks a fine up to RM10,000 and/or 1 year's imprisonment
vendors.typical_pricing: No registration fee; levy 1% of wages (10+ employees) or 0.5% (optional, 5–9).
vendors.my_specific_note: Under s.13(1) PSMB Act 2001, once you reach 10 Malaysian employees you must register and pay a 1%-of-wages levy (5–9 employees may opt in at 0.5%) — but a contractor can claw it back to fund CIDB Green Card and safety courses.
